features.title: QA Critical Fixes Wave 17
features.summary: Resolve latest QA-reported platform credibility, IA canonicalization, and conversion-flow blockers across runtime ops, guides routing, homepage, professionals listing, and profile contact UX.
acceptanceCriteria: Operational port strategy is consistent: QA/docs/automation and runtime agree on one serving port with no false-down endpoint.
Guides hub has one canonical route (either /odigoi or /blog) with 301 from duplicate and aligned nav/sitemap links.
Homepage exposes only customer-facing copy; no internal editorial/process text appears in production UI.
/professionals cards show only real, evidence-backed trust signals (no placeholder framework copy or zeroed synthetic metrics).
/professionals/[slug] hides empty portfolio media and exposes a single dominant contact path with secondary actions progressively disclosed.

tasks.directive: Primary QA endpoint is still broken: port 4322 refuses connections while the site only loads on 4321. Stop the operational chaos—either make 4322 reliably serve the app or update every QA instruction/automation to 4321 immediately.

You are publishing duplicate guide hub pages: /blog and /odigoi render essentially the same content/title, which creates SEO cannibalization and sloppy IA. Choose one canonical URL, 301 the duplicate, and align nav/sitemap/internal links accordingly.

Homepage still leaks internal editorial/instructional text into public UI (e.g. implementation-style copy blocks under hero). Remove all dev-note/process copy from production templates—users should only see customer-facing content.

/professionals still looks like fake staging data: cards expose placeholder-style identity/trust elements (e.g. initials tiles + generic 'framework' trust copy + zeroed metrics). Remove synthetic trust widgets from public cards until they are backed by real data and proof.

Professional profile page (/professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos) still ships an empty Portfolio container and a cluttered multi-form conversion stack. Hide empty media sections and reduce to one primary contact path instead of dumping competing forms in sequence.

features.title: QA Critical Fixes Wave 16
features.summary: Resolve latest QA-reported trust, profile UX, and truthful form-validation flow blockers across homepage, professionals, and form submission states.
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage hero contrast/typography is clearly readable on mobile/desktop and passes WCAG AA.
/professionals cards show no placeholder/filler trust language and hide zero-value trust widgets.
Professional profile pages hide empty media/portfolio sections and present one clear primary contact flow.
Invalid form submissions return truthful validation states (no faux-success redirects or HTTP 200 invalid flows).

tasks.directive: Homepage hero still fails basic readability: headline/body are ultra-light on a pale background (desktop + mobile), so the core value proposition is barely legible. Redesign hero contrast and typography hierarchy to pass WCAG AA and remove the glow haze that makes text look blurred.

/professionals still looks like staging data, not a trustworthy marketplace: cards expose labels like 'ΕΠΑΛΗΘΕΥΜΕΝΟ PLACEHOLDER' and trust metrics sitting at 0/100 across profiles. Remove all placeholder/filler language from public cards and hide credibility widgets until they are backed by real data.

Professional profile /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos still ships a broken credibility layout: large empty portfolio area and stacked conversion forms create visual clutter and low trust. Hide empty media sections and simplify to one primary contact flow instead of dumping multiple competing forms in sequence.

Form handling is misleading and opaque: invalid POSTs to /api/professionals/register return HTTP 200 and redirect users to a thank-you page with `status=invalid`, while other invalid form submits bounce to /professionals with query flags. Implement explicit validation UX with field-level errors and truthful success/failure states instead of faux-success redirects.

features.title: QA Critical Fixes Wave 15
features.summary: Resolve latest QA-reported public trust, hero readability, and profile credibility blockers across homepage and professionals surfaces.
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage contains no internal QA/dev-note instructional copy in public UI.
Homepage hero contrast/typography is readable on mobile/desktop and passes WCAG AA.
/professionals cards contain no placeholder labels and no demo-like avatar treatment.
/professionals trust blocks expose only meaningful public credibility signals.
Profile pages hide dead/empty portfolio media containers and show concise empty-state when no assets exist.

tasks.directive: Homepage is leaking internal QA/instructional copy into public UI (e.g. blocks like 'Πρώτη οθόνη με προτεραιότητα στην αναγνωσιμότητα...' under the hero). Remove all editorial/dev-note text from production templates immediately; users should never see implementation commentary.

Homepage hero remains unreadable on real devices: ultra-light heading/body text over pale gray with glow blur makes the core value proposition barely visible. Rebuild hero contrast/typography to WCAG AA and kill the haze effect so the message is legible in under 2 seconds.

/professionals still screams fake marketplace: profile cards display explicit labels like 'ΕΠΑΛΗΘΕΥΜΕΝΟ PLACEHOLDER' and initials-only avatar tiles. Strip placeholder language from public UI and replace with real media or clearly branded fallback components that don’t look like unfinished demo data.

/professionals cards are exposing internal trust-framework filler copy ('ΠΛΑΙΣΙΟ ΑΞΙΟΠΙΣΤΙΑΣ' with generic rules text) instead of user-useful proof. Replace this with real credibility signals (verified identity status, real review evidence, completed jobs) or hide the block until genuine data exists.

/professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos ships an empty 'Portfolio' container (large blank media area), which looks broken and unprofessional. If no assets exist, hide the section and show a concise empty-state; do not render dead visual blocks on live profile pages.

features.title: QA Critical Fixes Wave 14
features.summary: Resolve latest QA-reported credibility, compliance, messaging clarity, and localization blockers across professionals, registration, messages, and mobile hero.
acceptanceCriteria: Public professionals cards show only user-meaningful trust states (no placeholder labels or internal metrics).
Professionals listing hides zero-value/internal QA trust metrics until real data exists.
Professional registration includes mandatory GDPR consent with privacy-policy link before submit.
Public /messages empty state has one clear primary action with explicit outcome language.
Homepage/mobile hero copy is consistently localized in Greek (or controlled by real language switch).

tasks.directive: Σελίδα /professionals: οι κάρτες γράφουν ξεκάθαρα 'ΕΠΑΛΗΘΕΥΜΕΝΟ PLACEHOLDER'. Αυτό είναι αυτογκόλ αξιοπιστίας και δείχνει demo, όχι marketplace. Αφαίρεσε κάθε placeholder label από public UI και εμφάνισε μόνο πραγματική κατάσταση επαλήθευσης με καθαρό ελληνικό copy.

Σελίδα /professionals: εμφανίζεις 'Δείκτης εμπιστοσύνης 0/100' και 'Εκπαίδευση κριτικών: 0%' σε όλους τους επαγγελματίες. Αυτά μοιάζουν με εσωτερικά QA metrics και καταστρέφουν την εμπιστοσύνη του χρήστη. Κρύψε τα μέχρι να υπάρχουν πραγματικά δεδομένα και κράτα μόνο χρήσιμα δημόσια trust signals.

Σελίδα /professionals/register: η φόρμα εγγραφής επαγγελματία δεν έχει checkbox συναίνεσης ούτε σύνδεσμο σε πολιτική απορρήτου πριν το submit. Πρόσθεσε υποχρεωτή GDPR συναίνεση και σαφή ενημέρωση για χρήση/αποθήκευση στοιχείων πριν το κουμπί 'Αποστολή Αίτησης'.

Σελίδα /messages: στην κατάσταση χωρίς ενεργή συνομιλία δείχνεις παραπλανητικό primary CTA 'Συνέχεια' και δευτερεύον 'Δοκιμή ξανά' που δεν εξηγούν τι θα γίνει. Κράτα ένα ξεκάθαρο action ('Μετάβαση σε Επαγγελματίες') και γράψε ακριβές αποτέλεσμα ανά κουμπί για να μη νιώθει ο χρήστης ότι πατάει τυφλά.

Mobile homepage: το hero badge παραμένει στα αγγλικά ('GREEKHANDY SIGNATURE NETWORK') ενώ το υπόλοιπο UI είναι ελληνικό. Αυτή η γλωσσική ασυνέπεια δείχνει πρόχειρο template. Κάνε πλήρη ελληνική προσαρμογή στο hero (badge, headings, supporting copy) ή πρόσθεσε πραγματικό language switch με συνεπές localization.

features.title: QA Critical Fixes Wave 13
features.summary: Resolve latest QA blockers on hero readability, professionals trust credibility, broken profile service/portfolio UI blocks, and public messaging flow language/auth gating.
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage hero typography/colors pass WCAG AA and core value proposition is readable on desktop+mobile in first view.
/professionals cards avoid synthetic placeholder feel by using credible media fallbacks and stronger trust proof cues.
Profile service section UI corruption/overflow is removed and core service details are legible.
Profile portfolio section only renders valid media; empty/broken showcase states are hidden or replaced safely.
Public /messages no longer exposes internal jargon/diagnostics and uses clean Greek user-facing flow (or auth gate).

tasks.directive: Homepage hero is still visually broken on desktop and mobile: primary headline/body text is nearly invisible against the pale background, so users cannot read the core value proposition. Rebuild the hero color/typography system to pass WCAG AA contrast and make the message readable in under 2 seconds.

The /professionals grid still looks fake and templated: every card shows 'VERIFIED PLACEHOLDER' with initials-only avatars instead of credible identity. Replace synthetic placeholder presentation with real profile media or clearly branded fallback assets, and surface stronger trust proof (real review evidence, verification context, completed jobs).

Professional profile page /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos has a broken UI block in 'ΒΑΣΙΚΕΣ ΥΠΗΡΕΣΙΕΣ ΠΡΟΦΙΛ' (oversized blue pill/overlay with unreadable content). Fix layout/styles so service info is actually legible and remove any overflow artifact that makes the section look corrupted.

Same profile page ships unfinished portfolio visuals (empty/broken media blocks and generic placeholder avatar panel), which kills credibility for a paid marketplace. Require real portfolio assets per pro or hide the section until valid media exists; do not publish half-empty showcase components.

Public /messages page still exposes internal thread jargon and mixed-language UX ('MESSAGING ENTRY', 'thread id', 'viewer email δεν δόθηκε') to anonymous users. Replace this with a clean Greek user-facing flow (or gate behind authentication) and remove technical diagnostics from public copy.

features.title: QA Critical Fixes Wave 12
features.summary: Resolve latest QA-reported mobile hero readability, nav clarity, profile validation UX timing, runtime port consistency, and professional listing trust-signal quality blockers.
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage hero text/CTA contrast and typography are clearly readable on mobile and pass WCAG AA checks.
Mobile header has one clear navigation trigger with no duplicate menu label artifacts.
Profile booking/review/message validation errors appear only after interaction/submit with clean Greek microcopy.
Operational docs/automation/runtime port strategy is consistent and no longer creates 4321/4322 false-down reports.
Professional cards ship with stronger trust identity signals and deterministic photo/provenance fallback behavior.

tasks.directive: Mobile homepage still looks broken and low-trust: hero copy is nearly invisible (very light text on pale background), so the core value proposition is unreadable on first view. Rebuild mobile hero contrast/typography (background, heading, body, CTA states) to pass WCAG AA and make the message legible instantly.

Mobile header is still shipping duplicated navigation affordances ('ΜΕΝΟΥ' label + separate '☰ Μενού' button). Remove the redundant label and keep one clear, accessible trigger; right now it looks like unfinished UI and wastes above-the-fold space.

Professional profile page (`/professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos`) still shows inline validation error copy before meaningful user interaction (booking section warning text appears in default view). Do not dump error/validation messaging on initial load; trigger validation only after submit/field interaction and show clean user-facing microcopy.

Port strategy is still broken operationally: primary QA endpoint `:4322` is down while site serves on `:4321`. Either make `:4322` reliably live or officially switch all QA/docs/automation to `:4321`; this mismatch keeps generating false outage noise and wasted QA cycles.

Professional listing cards still feel synthetic and low-credibility (initials-only avatar tiles across all featured pros instead of real photos/proof). Upgrade listing trust signals: require real profile photos or branded verified placeholders with clear provenance, plus stronger identity cues (review proof, completed jobs, verification context).

features.name: QA Critical Fixes (Wave 9)
features.description: Wave 9 QA directives: remove/replace publicly broken /messages nav entry unless a real inbox entry flow exists; rebuild homepage hero contrast/typography hierarchy for clear WCAG AA readability; canonicalize contact IA by choosing either /epikoinonia or /#contact and redirect duplicate path; enforce hard professional media fallback/validation for broken listing cards (e.g., Giannis Kosta); and simplify professional profile conversion to one dominant CTA with secondary actions behind tabs/accordions.

features.name: QA Critical Fixes (Wave 8)
features.description: Wave 8 QA directives: ship complete mobile navigation (hamburger/drawer) so all primary routes are reachable on 375px; fix broken professional media card/hero for Γιάννης Κώστα with enforced pre-publish image validation + fallback thumbnails; reposition floating accessibility toolbar into a non-blocking edge trigger with collision-safe spacing; replace synthetic/templated category listing content with credible marketplace entries including distinct naming and proof signals; align heating taxonomy consistency so /thermansi slug, H1, metadata, and on-page copy express one coherent intent.

features.name: QA Critical Fixes (Wave 7)
features.description: Wave 7 QA directives: fix homepage hero readability/contrast to WCAG-compliant levels and remove haze blur; repair top-nav IA by routing Οδηγοί/Επαγγελματίες to live pages with automated nav-link 200 checks; align service taxonomy and slug routing so promoted categories (e.g. thermansi) never 404; improve category-card trust signals with validated marketplace data/provenance; add GDPR-grade consent + privacy notice and explicit success/failure/response-time expectations on category lead forms.

features.name: QA Critical Fixes (Wave 1)
features.description: Fix critical trust/UX/SEO issues from QA: broken professional images with robust fallbacks + publish validation gates, remove public-facing failure diagnostics and defer validation errors until user interaction, ensure complete OG/Twitter metadata, ship SEO-safe custom 404 UX, and improve homepage language consistency + information architecture (less endless-scroll fatigue).

features.name: QA Critical Fixes (Wave 2)
features.description: Wave 2 QA fixes: enforce single canonical production host and redirects from IP host, serve text/html; charset=utf-8 globally, resolve dead /blog and /epikoinonia routes with real pages or redirects, add singular/plural/transliteration slug aliases, and reduce homepage initial payload/link bloat with progressive disclosure.

features.name: QA Critical Fixes (Wave 3)
features.description: Wave 3 QA/founder directives: redesign homepage information architecture to reduce scroll fatigue while preserving SEO, fix homepage hero contrast/typography for WCAG readability (especially mobile), reposition/collapse floating accessibility toolbar so it never blocks core UI, replace thin /blog placeholder with real Greek editorial guides (author/date/reading-time) or demote from primary navigation, and restructure category pages (e.g. /ydravlikoi) with meaningful grouping + urgency/city/budget filtering and progressive disclosure.

features.name: QA Critical Fixes (Wave 4)
features.description: Wave 4 QA directives: stop public 500/TypeError crashes on /api/professionals/register, /api/reviews/submit, /api/bookings/submit, /api/messages/submit with robust server-side validation + safe 4xx field errors; strip Astro debug source-location attributes from production HTML; enforce clean branded production error handling with no Vite/dev artifacts; add JSON-LD schema.org coverage (Organization + WebSite homepage, Service category pages, LocalBusiness/ProfessionalService + aggregateRating profile pages).

features.name: QA Critical Fixes (Wave 5)
features.description: Wave 5 QA directives: remove production HTML debug/source-location leakage and aggressively cut homepage DOM/link bloat; restore missing <title> and meta description on homepage/profile templates; enforce UTF-8 end-to-end and add build/deploy mis-encoding guardrail for Greek copy; fix broken profile contact flow by wiring /api/bookings/submit and /api/messages/submit to real handlers (or correct form actions) with successful submission UX; re-fix professional image reliability on list/profile with publish-time media validation.

features.name: QA Critical Fixes (Wave 6)
features.description: Wave 6 QA directives: redesign mobile homepage hero color/typography/CTA system to pass WCAG AA contrast on real devices; fix broken professional media on listing/profile with hard fallbacks plus publish-time image fetch validation gates; reposition/collapse accessibility widget into non-blocking docked trigger; add practical category decision controls (city, urgency, budget, availability) and reduce initial card load; consolidate profile conversion UX into one primary CTA flow with secondary actions behind progressive disclosure.

features.name: Leads Directory Page
features.description: Create an internal leads page to view worker leads by category with related filters (city, status, score/search) for fast sourcing and outreach prep.

features.name: Search & Browse
features.description: Search bar on homepage, category grid with icons, city filter dropdown. Users can find services by keyword or browse categories.

features.name: Contact Forms
features.description: Per-service lead capture form with name, phone, email, description fields. Email notification to admin (SMTP-configured). Thank-you page after submission.

features.name: Professional Profiles
features.description: MVP slices complete: basic professional listing + profile pages + professional registration form submission + homepage search results include approved/published profiles + basic moderation workflow (pending queue with approve/reject and draft profile creation) + photos/portfolio display on profiles.

features.name: Premium Sitewide Experience
features.description: Airbnb-level premium visual and UX system across every surface: homepage, category pages, service pages, profile pages, booking flow, messaging threads, moderation/admin pages, and all states (empty/loading/error). Founder directive (2026-03-04): make the site feel world-class and non-templated with a unique GreekHandy brand identity; this is now the #1 priority.

features.name: Worker Lead Engine
features.description: Continuous worker/pro lead acquisition via subagents with extensive coverage by trade × city, deduplication/scoring, and daily high-priority outreach-ready lists.

features.name: Sitewide SEO Optimization
features.description: Continuous technical + on-page SEO improvements across homepage, category pages, service pages, profiles, schema metadata, internal linking, and performance signals to maximize rankings in Greece.

features.name: Reviews & Ratings
features.description: MVP slices complete: review submission form (1-5 rating + comment), pending moderation workflow, approved review display on professional profiles with average rating, and verified review badge shown only for published reviews tied to verified leads.

features.name: Booking System
features.description: Μικρότερο shippable MVP: end-to-end booking request flow από profile page (submit -> pending) με internal moderation queue και αποφάσεις approve/reject που αφαιρούν το request από τα pending.

features.name: Messaging
features.description: Smallest shippable Messaging MVP: one-to-one inquiry thread per lead (customer ↔ professional), plain-text messages, thread list with unread + last preview, and minimal moderation hooks (report message, block participant, admin hide message). Scope/acceptance: data/messaging-mvp.json.

features.name: Admin Dashboard
features.description: MVP slice scoped for immediate post-messaging launch: protected /admin shell + queue count overview + unified moderation queue for pending profiles, reviews, bookings, and reported messages. Single-item moderation actions only. Spec: data/admin-dashboard-mvp.json. [Founder directive 2026-03-04: paused/stopped.]

features.name: Payment System
features.description: Booking deposits via Stripe. Professional subscription plans. Invoice generation. Payment history.

features.title: QA Critical Fixes Wave 10
features.summary: Resolve latest QA-reported UX/content blockers affecting homepage, profiles, service pages, and accessibility overlays.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile header has a single clear menu trigger with consistent spacing.
Public profile pages no longer expose technical/validation debug copy.
Service pages never show empty related-services sections.
Accessibility toolbar no longer overlaps page CTAs/content.
Homepage hero contrast/typography passes WCAG AA readability expectations.

tasks.directive: Mobile homepage header is sloppy and confusing: it shows both a standalone 'ΜΕΝΟΥ' label and a separate '☰ Μενού' button at the same time. Remove the duplicate label and ship one clear mobile navigation trigger with consistent spacing/alignment.

Professional profile page `/professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos` still leaks internal validation/debug copy directly into public UI (e.g. inline technical warnings in booking/reviews sections). Stop exposing system-style error text to users; show clean Greek microcopy and only after user interaction/submit.

Service page `/ydravlikoi` has an empty 'Σχετικές Υπηρεσίες' section ('Δεν υπάρχουν σχετικές υπηρεσίες για την ώρα'), which makes the page feel unfinished. Either populate real related services dynamically or remove the section entirely until it has content.

Floating accessibility toolbar is still physically covering page content/controls across key pages (`/blog`, `/allagi-kleidarias`, `/ydravlikoi`, profile pages). Dock it to a non-overlapping edge trigger and enforce collision-safe spacing so it never sits on top of CTAs/text.

Homepage hero is still barely readable (very light text on pale background, especially on mobile), so first impression looks broken. Rebuild hero contrast and typography hierarchy to pass WCAG AA and make core value proposition legible instantly.

features.title: QA Critical Fixes Wave 11
features.summary: Resolve latest QA-reported technical SEO, performance, metadata grammar, and runtime/ops consistency blockers.
acceptanceCriteria: No Astro source-path debug attributes are present in served HTML.
Homepage/category HTML payloads are materially reduced via progressive disclosure and capped initial render volume.
/robots.txt and /sitemap.xml return HTTP 200 with valid content and indexable URL coverage.
Location/service page titles and headings never duplicate city phrases.
Port strategy is consistent across runtime and operational docs (4321/4322 mismatch resolved).

tasks.directive: Stop shipping Astro debug source attributes to production/dev preview. Every page is leaking `data-astro-source-file` paths (e.g. `/home/weakwire/projects/greekhandy/...`) and bloating HTML massively. Disable source annotations in build output and strip all `data-astro-source-*` attributes before deploy.

Homepage and major category pages are absurdly heavy HTML payloads (homepage ~1.08MB, /ydravlikoi ~1.78MB before assets). This is unacceptable for mobile users on Greek networks. Cut server-rendered markup size aggressively: paginate/limit long lists, lazy-render below-the-fold blocks, and remove duplicated hidden content.

Fix broken local SEO infrastructure: `/robots.txt` and `/sitemap.xml` both return 404. Add valid robots and sitemap endpoints immediately, include all indexable pages, and verify they return 200 with correct content-type.

Your location template has a grammar duplication bug in metadata and headings (e.g. `στην Πάτρα στην Πάτρα`, `στην Αθήνα στην Αθήνα` on pages like `/allagi-diakopton-prizon-patra` and `/episkevi-diarrois-stegi-amesa-athina`). Refactor title/meta generation to prevent repeated city injection and re-generate affected pages.

Port 4322 is down while QA instructions and agent workflow point to it as primary. Align runtime with documented port strategy: either make 4322 reliably serve the site or update all operational docs/automation to 4321. Right now this creates false downtime and wasted QA cycles.

milestones.name: MVP Features
milestones.target: Search, contact forms, basic profiles working

milestones.name: Premium Experience Rollout
milestones.target: Entire site (public + internal) upgraded to premium Airbnb-level look/feel with consistent design system and conversion-first UX

milestones.name: Extensive Lead Coverage
milestones.target: Always-on lead subagents across major trades and cities, with broad coverage, scoring, and daily prioritized lead outputs

milestones.name: Marketplace v1
milestones.target: Reviews, booking, messaging live + premium UX baseline + lead engine operational
